meaning: n.(家禽,牲畜的)存活率,(住宅,环境的)适于居住性
examples: “The intellectual energy of a city depends on livability,” he says.
The quality of life and livability of these areas is really heartbreaking.
It was wounding for a city that prides itself on hospitality and livability.
He's arguing that such amenities are what perennially put the state near the top of livability lists.
In the category of livability, however, bad infrastructure for public transport is one of the Auckland's largest detractions.
